\paragraph{Results summary.}
Across 250 simulation steps (example\_water\_nacl), the coarse-grained free energy evolved from $-80.14$ to $-121.7$ (minimum $-121.7$). The electrolyte layer reports ionic strength $I = 0.04982$ (mol per liter) and Debye screening length $\lambda_D = 1.363$~nm. Spectral indicators yield a Dirac gap of $0.295$ (simulator units). Braid complexity (reduced word length) reaches $247$. These metrics are qualitative graph-level proxies; quantitative agreement with experiment is not implied.
